steps.title: Identify the core connection
steps.content: Knight imitated Hair's fast painting technique—the exact method the passage says creates the impressionistic qualities synonymous with the group's aesthetic.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly follows: if Knight adopted the technique that creates the group's characteristic style, his paintings embody that style—more so than Reagan's, who emphasized detail over speed.

The remaining choices either contradict Knight's documented behavior, invent unsupported quality judgments, or make comparisons the passage never establishes.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
